name: CERT: Static Analysis Before Commit
description: Source is checked against the CERT C / C++ ruleset by static analysis before it is committed.
rationale: SEI CERT conformance: an analyzer diagnoses rule violations; conformance is established by analysis (plus manual review for undecidable rules) before code is accepted.
source: SEI CERT Coding Standards (Carnegie Mellon Software Engineering Institute) — CERT C and CERT C++ Secure Coding Standards, with the CERT risk-assessment methodology (Severity × Likelihood × Remediation Cost → Priority → Level L1/L2/L3). https://wiki.sei.cmu.edu/confluence/display/seccode
